category_title: Struggling Chamorro readers and writers in collaborative settings
minimal_context: The students are in a small group practicing reading and retelling the tale 'The Old Lady in the Vinegar Bottle' to one another.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time to practice reading and retelling 'The Old Lady in the Vinegar Bottle' to one another in your small group.

STUDENT: It's not your turn, it's my turn, it's not your turn.

TUTOR: Please decide who will go first.

STUDENT: Okay whose turn? Your turn.

TUTOR: Listen quietly while he begins to retell the story.

STUDENT: Juan, you're supposed to start from the beginning.

options.A: Provide the correct articulation of the student's statement, ensuring the words are pronounced clearly during the small group retelling activity.
options.B: Enforce the rule against interrupting the reteller, reminding the student to wait until after the retelling is completed to provide constructive feedback.
options.C: Respond with enthusiastic praise for the student's participation, saying, 'Another one already? No way! I’m so proud of you! Good job, keep going.'
options.D: Use a leading question to prompt the student to continue the story, asking, 'Juan started from the beginning, and then what happened?'
